description: All rough electrical boxes, wiring, panel rough-in, and low-voltage blocking are installed, verified against plans, and ready for Baldwin County rough electrical inspection before insulation or drywall begins.

categories.name: Plans & Layout Verification
items: Approved electrical plans on-site and referenced by electrician for every box location
Lighting fixture schedule cross-referenced against framed ceiling and wall locations before wire pull
Panel location confirmed per plans; clearance in front of panel verified at minimum 36 inches per NEC 110.26
All specialty circuit locations (kitchen appliances, EV charger, generator transfer switch, pool/spa sub-panel) marked on framing before rough-in begins
Low-voltage rough-in drawing (structured wiring, security, audio/video, Cat 6) confirmed with A/V and security subs before wire pull

categories.name: Rough-In Work
items: All outlet, switch, and fixture boxes nailed at correct AFF height (outlets 16" AFF standard; switches 48" AFF unless otherwise specified on plans) and plumb within 1/8 inch
Box fill calculations verified per NEC 314.16 — no box overfilled with conductors, devices, or clamps
All home runs pulled to panel in correct wire gauge: 15A circuits #14 AWG, 20A circuits #12 AWG, 30A+ circuits sized per load calculations on approved plans
Dedicated 20A circuits confirmed for each kitchen small-appliance circuit, refrigerator, dishwasher, disposal, and microwave per NEC 210.11
AFCI protection confirmed for all bedroom, living, dining, hallway, and kitchen branch circuits per NEC 210.12 (2020 NEC as adopted by Alabama)
GFCI protection confirmed at all required locations: bathrooms, garage, exterior, kitchen within 6 ft of sink, crawl space, unfinished basement, and pool/spa equipment per NEC 210.8
Wire secured within 12 inches of every box and every 4.5 feet along run; no loose wire draped across framing members
All wire penetrations through studs and top/bottom plates within 1.25 inches of edge protected with nail plates

categories.name: Panel & Service Rough-In
items: Main panel (or sub-panel) box mounted plumb and level, properly fastened to framing, and sized per load calculation on approved plans
Panel schedule drafted and taped inside panel door — every breaker slot labeled to circuit and location
Service entrance conduit stubbed to exterior in correct location and height coordinated with Baldwin EMC for meter base placement
Grounding electrode system installed per NEC 250: ground rods driven, ground wire landed, and inspector-ready
Generator transfer switch rough-in complete if on scope; interlock or ATS wired and labeled
EV charger circuit (typically 50A/240V) rough-in complete to garage location, conduit stubbed if charger is owner-furnished

categories.name: Low-Voltage & Specialty Systems
items: Structured media panel (home-run location) confirmed, mounted, and all Cat 6, coax, and speaker wire home-runs landed
Security system rough-in complete: door/window sensor wires, motion zones, and keypad locations wired per security sub's drawing
Audio/video rough-in complete: speaker wire, HDMI conduit, and TV backing (3/4" plywood or blocking) installed at every specified location
Smoke detector locations set at all required positions per NFPA 72 and Alabama Residential Code: each bedroom, outside each sleeping area, each level
CO detector rough-in locations confirmed per Alabama Residential Code requirements
Doorbell, exterior camera, and landscape lighting conduits stubbed to correct locations and capped

categories.name: Coordination & Pre-Inspection Checks
items: Plumbing and HVAC rough-in complete before electrical inspection is called — all trades above ceiling and in walls finished so inspector can walk full scope
All wire ends labeled or tagged at panel end with circuit number matching panel schedule
Superintendent walked all rooms with electrical plans and verified box count, circuit assignments, and specialty locations before calling inspection
Any field deviations from approved plans documented as RFIs, reviewed with electrician, and noted on as-built markup before inspection
Attic and crawl space wiring inspected by superintendent for proper support, no insulation contact where not rated, and no splices outside of listed boxes

categories.name: Inspection & Pre-Cover Sign-Off
items: Baldwin County rough electrical inspection scheduled and passed — inspection card signed and posted on-site
Any inspection corrections fully remediated and re-inspected before insulation is approved to proceed
Electrical contractor signed off on rough-in completion in writing (email or Buildertrend field log)
Superintendent confirmed no open junction boxes, missing box covers, or exposed splices remain before insulation begins
Photos of panel rough-in, all specialty circuit runs, and any concealed conduit uploaded to project Drive folder as permanent record before walls close
